model_interpretation.explanation: NPS detraction appears EVENT-DRIVEN rather than PROFILE-DRIVEN. The model barely outperforms baseline, meaning customer demographics and transaction history alone cannot reliably predict who will be a detractor. Specific experiences or service events likely trigger dissatisfaction.

recommendations.finding: Very high engagement customers have 20.1% detractor rate vs 11.4% for low engagement — a 8.7pp gap.
recommendations.action: Implement proactive account management for customers above $200k total transactions. Assign dedicated reps, conduct quarterly satisfaction checks before NPS surveys.
recommendations.expected_impact: Reducing very_high detractor rate by 5pp could save significant revenue from churn risk.

recommendations.finding: Seller-only accounts are 17.6% detractors vs 13.1% for buyer-only — sellers are 1.3x more likely to be detractors.
recommendations.action: Investigate seller-specific pain points: fee transparency, settlement timing, lot condition reporting, buyer's premium communication. Classify detractor comments from seller-only accounts to identify top themes.
recommendations.expected_impact: Seller satisfaction directly impacts consignment pipeline and repeat business.

recommendations.finding: Detractors average 14.0% win rate vs 14.85% for promoters. Losing more auctions correlates with lower satisfaction.
recommendations.action: Consider 'second chance' offers, bid coaching/alerts, or personalized item recommendations to improve win rates for frequent losers.
recommendations.expected_impact: Improving bidder success rate addresses a root cause of dissatisfaction.

recommendations.finding: The logistic regression model shows NPS detraction is event-driven, not profile-driven. Customer demographics and history alone cannot predict who will be a detractor.
recommendations.action: Shift from profile-based risk scoring to event-based monitoring. Track service touchpoints (title delays, condition disputes, fee surprises) and flag accounts experiencing negative events for proactive outreach.
recommendations.expected_impact: Event-based intervention catches dissatisfaction at the moment it happens, before it becomes an NPS detractor response.

top_detractors.company_name: A C D C INVESTMENTS LLC

top_detractors.nps_comment: I have been pleased with most purchases until the last one that we purchased a 2007 Peterbilt from Collinsville OK.  The auction yard left it out of the because I could not get there  by closing time, which was nice.  However, the truck batteries were completely dead, so dead that 2 sets of jumper cables would not start it, it still had enough air pressure that I was able to have my wife pull me with my pickup to get it started.  Then the clutch was so still that I had to put my foot under the pedal to pull it up to engage it.  I don' mind those things but am very troubled by the fact that it was not disclosed and am disappointed in Purple Wave for that.  Always in past experiences, Purple wave has be very accurate with inspections and reporting flaws.  I know that the batteries did not just die that dead in one day and the clutch did not get that stiff in one day.  I was able to get it home but my wife had to pull me to start it 3 times.
you folks used to list everything we had to sell, now you are being selective and not listing everything. As a government agency , this is not helpful when trying to dispose of property.
Banned from buying and never giving a reason.  And no one would ever call me back and give a reason
Buyers fee is too high
your buyers fee is to expensive I always factor it in when I bid.

I am still wondering why my account has been haulted? I did nothing to have this happen. Not sure why the account was fined for something I didnâ€™t do. So please let me know what I need to do to resolve this. I understand that the items I listed were listed correctly and were titled in my name. I couldnâ€™t bid on them as the account wouldnâ€™t let me.
Have a cap on your 10% buyers fee.
The 10% buyer’s premium with no cap makes buying big thicket item cost prohibitive.  You need to put a $1,000 cap on the buyers fee!!!
I have been buying and selling with you for years then I try to bid and I'm cut off
They do not put the correct descriptions, you guys help the seller more than the buyer.
Need to do a better job at vetting buyers. Still waiting over a month for a buyer to pay.
If they ask, I'll suggest.
Getting paid for items sold takes way too long and often they dont settle on time . The last time I was shorted 8k ! and that was 40 days after it was sold

You complexly failed me when I used you to sell my equipment in that you did not even come close to what you told me the range of my equip would sell for. But I consider you a good place to get equipment below market value.

I I don’t think you’re very honest company. I passed experience with you was not good. I know you’ve plugged the bid on several occasions run me up because I put my max bid in and that’s exactly what I bought it for.
It takes to long to receive titles when you pay properly it shouldnâ€™t take over thirty days to receive title
75% of the items I have purchased were 100% misrepresented and were junk !  If I had the time and energy I’d sue PW and all of their con artist consignors !
There is no cap on your buyer's premium
I don’t like the buyers premium
